creator: bartsch@dssgmbh.de
text:
-----BEGIN PGP SIGNED MESSAGE-----
Hash: SHA1

Am 14.09.2012 08:48, schrieb Alfred Bartsch:
> Am 13.09.2012 21:42, schrieb Bryan Drewery:
>> This works for me using 9.0 i386. What release/arch are you 
>> building for?
> 
> 
> I'm building for 8.3-RELEASE, both amd64 and i386. These jails are
> freshly created with poudriere-2.0.0.
> 
>> ====>> [01] Starting build of devel/libftdi ====>> [02] Starting 
>> build of comms/libirman ====>> [02] Finished build of 
>> comms/libirman: Success ====>> [02] Starting build of 
>> textproc/odt2txt ====>> [02] Finished build of textproc/odt2txt: 
>> Success ====>> [02] Starting build of textproc/iksemel ====>>
>> [01] Finished build of devel/libftdi: Success ====>> [01]
>> Starting build of comms/lirc ====>> [02] Finished build of
>> textproc/iksemel: Success ====>> [01] Finished build of
>> comms/lirc: Success ====>> Stopping 2 builders ====>> Creating
>> pkgng repository tar: Removing leading '/' from member names
>> Generating repo.sqlite in /usr/ports/packages/: done! ====>>
>> Cleaning up ====>> Umounting file systems ====>> 5 packages
>> built, 0 failures, 0 ignored, 0 skipped ====>> Built ports:
>> comms/libirman textproc/odt2txt devel/libftdi textproc/iksemel
>> comms/lirc
> 

I repeated building textproc/odt2txt with an up-to-date ports tree.
This did not help.
Finally, upgrading poudriere from 2.0.0 to 2.1.2 solves this issue.

Now this port builds with poudriere flawlessly. Thanks.

- -- 
Regards
Alfred Bartsch
Data-Service GmbH
